title: Bribery at elections
content: Whoever gives a gratification to any person with the object of inducing him or any other person to exercise any electoral right or of rewarding any person for having exercised any such right... commits the offence of bribery.

illumination_lamp.scenario: A gives money to B to induce B to vote for a specific candidate.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: A is guilty of bribery at an election under Section 171B.

footnotes.text: Electoral Bribery: Includes both active giving and passive accepting of any economic or non-economic favor to alter a vote or candidacy choice.

title: Undue influence at elections
content: Whoever voluntarily interferes or attempts to interfere with the free exercise of any electoral right commits the offence of undue influence at an election.

illumination_lamp.scenario: A threatens B that if B votes for a certain candidate, B will face social excommunication or spiritual displeasure.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: A has committed the offence of undue influence under Section 171C.

media_tutor.title: Offences Relating to Elections - Legal Analysis

footnotes.text: Interference Test: Threatening a voter with injury, social excommunication, or spiritual censure/divine displeasure constitutes complete statutory undue influence under this section.
